name: Process Creation
description: Refers to the event in which a new process (executable) is initialized by an operating system. This can involve parent-child process relationships, process arguments, and environmental variables. Monitoring process creation is crucial for detecting malicious behaviors, such as execution of unauthorized binaries, scripting abuse, or privilege escalation attempts.

event_logs.description: Audit Process Creation; captures process creation with associated parent process.

sysmon.description: Process Creation; provides detailed logging including hashes and command lines.

edr.description: Provide process telemetry, tracking execution flows and arguments.

low_level_monitoring.description: Used for low-level monitoring of system calls related to process execution.

query_tools.description: Allows SQL-like queries to track process events.

tools.name: Zeek (Bro)
tools.description: Captures network-based process execution related to remote shells.

tools.name: Syslog/OSSEC
tools.description: Tracks execution of processes on distributed systems.

examples: Monitor process creation for uncommon binaries in user directories.
Detect processes with suspicious command-line arguments.
